name: Dilly Brunch Pockets
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Plus 2 tablespoons Sliced green onion
1 teaspoon Chopped fresh dill OR 1/4 teaspoon Dried dill
1 Package Canadian Bacon (5 to 6 Ounces) or 12 thin slices Folded in half
recipeInstructions:
To warm and fold corn tortillas, briefly cook each tortilla on both sides in a nonstick skillet, about 1 minute. Do not overcook. Fold in quarters. Keep prepared tortillas covered with cloth towel until all tortillas are folded and ready to be filled.
In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, briefly saute tomato and 2/4 cup green onions with dill in margarine. Add beaten eggs, salt, and pepper to taste. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally until eggs are just cooked but still soft. Remove from heat.
To fill each tortilla, place one slice of Canadian bacon in one "pocket" of folded tortilla. Fill second "pocket" with about 2 tablespoons egg mixture. Arrange overlapping filled tortillas in a shallow 12x8x2 inch baking dish (lightly brushed with margarine or sprayed with nonstick cooking spray). Sprinkle with cheese, if desired, and remaining 2 tablespoons chopped green onion. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes or until just heated through.
